front: Author: title essay of a collection about the Haight-Ashbury district opens 'The center was not holding'; meets a five-year-old girl given acid and peyote ('High Kindergarten')
Author: essay about disillusionment with New York; notes 'it is distinctly possible to stay too long at the Fair' and 'six months can become eight years with the deceptive ease of a film dissolve'
Author: essay about Lucille Miller's conviction for murdering her husband in a burning car in the San Bernardino Valley
back: Some Dreamers of the Golden Dream (Joan Didion)

front: Author: essay collection whose title essay opens 'We tell ourselves stories in order to live'; calls The Doors 'the Norman Mailers of the Top Forty'
Author: title essay of a collection describes conversations with Manson follower Linda Kasabian and visits to Eldridge Cleaver's apartment and Black Panther meetings
Author: grief memoir about her husband's death; features the recurring line 'You sit down to dinner and life as you know it ends' and the inability to give away his shoes
back: The Year of Magical Thinking (Joan Didion)

front: Novel: the institutionalized Hollywood actress Maria Wyeth writes 'NOTHING APPLIES' on her psychological test and compulsively drives the freeway; begins with 'What makes Iago evil?'
back: Play It As It Lays (Joan Didion)

front: Novel: ends with the protagonist saying 'I know what nothing means, and keep on playing'; BZ's suicide leads to her institutionalization
back: Play It As It Lays (Joan Didion)

front: Author: memoir about her daughter Quintana Roo's death; 'When we talk about mortality we are talking about our children'
Author: co-wrote screenplays with her husband including The Panic in Needle Park (Al Pacino's breakthrough) and A Star is Born (1976)
Author: essay recalls visiting the set of a John Wayne movie; 'deep in that part of my heart where the artificial rain forever falls'
Author: defined writing as 'a hostile act' in a talk whose title was 'stolen' from George Orwell
